canonical_question: What insurance do startups need by stage and industry — policies, cost benchmarks?
aliases: Startup insurance requirements checklist by funding stage
How much does startup business insurance cost per year?
Which insurance policies do VCs require before investing?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2025 cyber insurance premiums stabilized after 2022-2023 spike; D&O rates softened 5-10% in soft market

constraints: Workers' compensation is legally required in 49 states once you hire your first W-2 employee — Texas is the only exception
D&O insurance is effectively mandatory before accepting VC funding — most term sheets include it as a closing condition
Insurance requirements vary by state — always verify state-specific thresholds for workers' comp, auto, and professional liability
Cost benchmarks are US-market medians — international startups face different regulatory requirements and pricing
Policy limits must match contractual obligations — enterprise customers often require $1M-$5M in coverage as a vendor condition

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs legal entity formation guidance, not insurance
User needs employee benefits strategy beyond insurance

inputs_needed.question: What is the startup's current funding stage?
What industry is the startup in?
How many W-2 employees does the startup have?
In which US state is the startup incorporated or primarily operating?

depends_on.label: Entity type determines insurance eligibility and requirements

related_to.label: D&O insurance protects equity holders and board members
